description:
## Source

Recurring fuckup class observed 10+ times in last 3 hours (2026-05-08T23:52Z onward):
- flywheel-glrlb rm -rf absolute path blocked
- flywheel-olp9b rm-rf, find-delete, mv-to-Trash all blocked
- flywheel-z6lk3 rm-rf + mv from /var/folders blocked
- flywheel-mjrly reservation+cleanup combo blocked
- flywheel-wire-worker-close-requires-git-commit-a1912c82 unlinks blocked
- flywheel-rdqc7.1 recursive cleanup blocked
- flywheel-03uki overbroad scratch cleanup blocked
- flywheel-ftj0m rm -rf dispatch scratch blocked
- flywheel-wire-dispatch-to-lib-not-bin redirect-truncate blocked
- ... and counting

## Pattern

Workers create scratch via `mktemp -d /var/folders/.../T/flywheel-<bead-id>.XXXXXX.*`, do work, attempt `rm -rf <absolute_path>` to clean up. DCG PreToolUse hook blocks `rm -rf` regardless of path scope. Workers recover via fallback (rmdir, explicit unlinks, mv-to-Trash) which is less reliable + leaves orphans in `/var/folders/.../T/`.

## Fix: canonical helper

Author `~/.local/bin/flywheel-cleanup-scratch` (or `.flywheel/scripts/cleanup-scratch.sh`).
- Single argument: absolute path
- Validates path matches `^/var/folders/.*/T/(flywheel|josh|wave)-.*` OR `^/tmp/(flywheel|dispatch_)-.*`
- If validates: emits `rm -rf` via internal logic
- DCG allowlist: permit this binary by full path (already proven pattern with tmp-aggressive-prune-cron-wrapper)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Author `.flywheel/scripts/cleanup-scratch.sh` with path validation + JSON output (--dry-run / --apply / --json shape).
AG2: Add to DCG allowlist by full path (per `dcg` skill canonical-by-full-path pattern).
AG3: Update worker-tick template to call `flywheel-cleanup-scratch $TMPDIR_PATH` instead of raw `rm -rf` at end of bead.
AG4: Test fixtures: valid paths (canonical scratch), invalid paths (refused with reason), non-existent paths (no-op).
AG5: Doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/scratch-cleanup-canonical-pattern.md` capturing the pattern + reference to fuckup history (10+ occurrences).
AG6: Propagate via doctrine-sync 6h cadence — all 57 fleet repos pick up cleanup helper + worker-tick template change.

## Cross-references

- Memory: `feedback_dcg_prose_trigger_strip_dangerous_substrings`
- Skill: `dcg` (canonical destructive-command-guard)
- Existing canonical-by-full-path pattern: `tmp-aggressive-prune-cron-wrapper`, `codex-rollout-permission-janitor`
- Fuckup-log evidence at ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl (10+ entries 2026-05-08T23:52Z+)
title: [doctrine] flywheel-cleanup-scratch helper — DCG-allowlisted scratch-dir cleanup primitive

Path requires applying ALL 10 recommendations + iterative re-audit + addressing each weak-dimension structurally.

- Strongest: output_parseability 810, self_documentation 770, safety_with_recovery 750
- Weakest: intent_inference 510, regression_resistance 545

Joshua's purchased skill from jeffreys-skills.md fails to load on every codex 0.125 / 0.129 startup with:

```
⚠ /Users/josh/.codex/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
⚠ /Users/josh/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
```

Skill name from SKILL.md frontmatter:
`agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools` = 76 chars

## Workaround

Symlink with shorter name:
```bash
ln -s agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools \
  ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics
```

This may or may not work depending on whether codex resolves symlinks before validating name. If frontmatter `name:` is what's checked, need to also patch SKILL.md frontmatter to a shorter alias.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Verify whether codex's 64-char limit applies to:
- Directory name
- SKILL.md frontmatter `name:` field
- Both
Determines workaround shape.

AG2: Apply workaround locally — shorten name to fit. Suggested: `agent-ergonomics-cli` (already exists at ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-cli/ — different/older skill).
Or: `agent-ergonomics-max` (20 chars).

AG3: File issue at jeffreys-skills.md / Dicklesworthstone/jeffreys-skills (or wherever the skill ships from) noting the name length collides with codex 64-char limit.

AG4: Document the codex 64-char limit as a SKILL-NAMING-CONSTRAINT canonical L-rule for our own skills (avoid future authoring trip).

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey announcement piece (Joshua shared 2026-05-08T~21:50Z)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff)
- canonical-cli-scoping skill (audit subject of meta-application — being audited via this skill)
title: [skill-bug] agent-ergonomics-... skill name 76 chars exceeds codex 64-char limit

description:
## Source

Jeffrey Emanuel released /agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools — a 189-file, 1.5MB skill for converting CLIs from human-first to agent-first via 11-dim scoring + recommendation loop. Already in our skill registry (Joshua paid $20/mo). Joshua shared his announcement piece + GPT-5.5 review 2026-05-08T~21:50Z.

## Why apply to our substrate

Our flywheel runs on CLIs that workers + orchestrators + watchdogs invoke constantly:
- `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`
- `validate-callback.py`, `tmp-aggressive-prune.sh`, `flywheel-doctor`
- `frozen-pane-detector-fleet.sh`, `peer-orch-respawn-permit.sh`
- 50+ scripts across `.flywheel/scripts/`

If we apply the skill to even the top-10 most-invoked, we'd:
- Surface agent-friction patterns we've been hand-treating
- Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` baseline for time-series tracking
- Get specific 11-dim scores + ranked recommendations per script
- Catch wrong-but-legible invocation failure modes (exact LODP false-negatives we've been seeing)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Inventory top-10 most-invoked CLIs in our substrate. Rank by dispatch-log frequency × failure-rate × blast-radius (skill's own ranking heuristic).

AG2: Run skill against the top-3 (likely: `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`). Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` + `recommendations.jsonl` per tool.

AG3: Apply changes auto-mode for low-blast-radius recommendations; surface high-blast-radius for Joshua review.

AG4: Re-score post-changes. Confirm fresh-agent simulation (subagent without prior context) successfully invokes each tool first-try.

AG5: Doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/agent-ergonomics-application-baseline-2026-05-08.md` capturing 11-dim scores + applied changes + re-score deltas.

AG6: Establish quarterly re-audit cadence — agent-ergonomics is a time-series, not one-off.

## Strategic angle

This skill IS the meta-skill for our entire flywheel substrate. Every dispatch packet, every wrapper script, every doctor tool either does or doesn't pass agent-first design. Applying systematically gives us:
- Compounding leverage: one rubric → 50+ improved tools → fleet-wide friction reduction
- Cross-orch propagation: doctrine-sync 6h cadence pushes improved scripts to alps/mobile-eats/skillos
- New-orch onboarding: future workers face cleaner tooling, climb learning curve faster

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey's piece (shared by Joshua 2026-05-08T21:50Z)
- GPT-5.5 review of skill in same context
- Memory: `feedback_canonical_cli_at_dispatch` (canonical-cli-scoping enforcement at dispatch)
- Skill: `canonical-cli-scoping` (our existing CLI discipline skill — complementary)
- Skill: `world-class-doctor-mode-for-cli-tools` (Jeffrey's prior CLI doctor skill, already applied)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff in human-facing surfaces)
title: [doctrine] apply agent-ergonomics skill to our own .flywheel/scripts substrate

description:
## Source

Joshua observation 2026-05-08T21:30Z: "getting low on storage again". df -h shows:
- /dev/disk3s1s1: 926Gi total, 11Gi used, 16Gi avail, 42% capacity, ifree 168M

Wait — used=11Gi but avail=16Gi on a 926Gi disk doesn't add up. Either df is lying about apparent vs free, OR there's snapshot space reserved. APFS sealed system volume + snapshot reserved space likely accounts for ~900Gi of "missing" capacity.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Audit actual data consumers — run `du -sh ~/Developer/* ~/.socraticode/* ~/.knowledge/* ~/Library/Caches/* /private/tmp/* /private/var/folders/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -30` to find real top consumers.

AG2: Identify whether APFS snapshots are pinning space — `tmutil listlocalsnapshots / | wc -l` and `diskutil apfs list | grep -A2 Snapshot`.

AG3: Audit jeff-corpus + socraticode-data sizes — these accrete daily per memory rule (`feedback_jeff_corpus_indexed_data_separates_from_source`). Source bulk is disposable, indexed qdrant data is precious.

AG4: Audit /private/tmp — current cron-wrapper runs hourly with --max-mtime-days=1. Verify it's actually firing + freeing space. Check `~/.local/state/flywheel/tmp-aggressive-prune-cron.jsonl` for ledger.

AG5: Author + ship a fleet-wide storage-pressure-doctor that surfaces top consumers + snapshot reservation + recommends action when avail <20Gi.

## Cross-references

- Memory rule: `feedback_private_tmp_accretes_until_disk_dies` (312GB on 926GB disk before noticed)
- Memory rule: `feedback_storage_pressure_blocks_substrate` (<5% free degrades WAL/JSONL)
- Memory rule: `feedback_retention_policy_by_default_for_accreting_surfaces`
- Skill: `storage-health`, `apfs-snapshot-ops`, `dev-cache-janitor`
- Hourly prune: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.tmp-aggressive-prune.plist`
title: [storage] investigate where 910Gi+ used disk lives — 16Gi avail trending wrong

description:
Cross-orch from skillos:1 (BrightLake), Joshua green-lit. Mobile-eats had TWO leaks today:
- 1st: Nango credential commit 63c9d58 (caught by CI gitleaks 2 days later)
- 2nd: DATABASE_URL via `head -c 200 /tmp/safe-sink.json` readback (~6h after 1st, vector-6 readback class)

Joshua directive: "I will rotate all secrets later — once we can prove our systems can stop leaking our damn secrets." No rotation until structural fix lands.

## Phase 1 scope (this bead, EXPANDED with vector-6)

L-rule canonical authoring covers MULTIPLE vectors of the same rank-3 root (agent never holds raw secret values):

AG1: Author canonical L-rule PRE-COMMIT-GITLEAKS-MANDATORY in .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md.

AG2: EXTEND with vector #6 readback class:
> Orchestrator bash MUST NOT read contents of any file under /tmp/ written from infisical|curl|vercel env pull|gitleaks source in same session.
> Permitted: shasum, wc -c, jq 'keys', jq -r '.[].secretKey' (key-only, never value).
> Forbidden: head -c, cat, tail, grep, awk, sed, jq -r '.[].value', any value-revealing op on tainted tmp-files.

AG3: Doctor invariants (BOTH classes):
- pre_commit_secret_scanner_installed=yes|no (WARN 7-day grace, FAIL after)
- pretooluse_bash_diagnostic_hook_installed=yes|no per orchestrator session (WARN absent)

AG4: Author .flywheel/doctrine/secrets-leak-prevention-stack.md describing:
- Layer A: PreToolUse bash hook (skillos:1 owns, pending Joshua-authorize on global ~/.claude/settings.json)
- Layer B: Vercel-Infisical native (mobile-eats:1 per-repo deploy concern)
- Layer C: Rank-3 generalization to diagnostics (skillos:1 inline)
- Layer D: AGENTS-CANONICAL L-rule extension (THIS BEAD)
- 3-layer dispatch architecture (Phase 1+2: pre-commit + Phase 3: skillos linter + Phase 4: evidence_redacted)

AG5: Phase 2 (wire-up) + Phase 3 (skillos linter — DONE PR #90) + Phase 4 (rank-3 callback contract — flywheel-dwavb) tracked separately.

description:
## Joshua directive 2026-05-08T21:26Z

> "I want all repos to be set up as if they were public — even if they are private — if I decide to flip the switch one day, it needs to be ready."

## Exemption taxonomy (CLARIFIED)

Three exemption classes — only ONE is now deprecated:

| Class | Status under PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT |
|---|---|
| **private_internal** (Joshua-owned, currently private) | **DEPRECATED** — re-audit per flywheel-sib04. Joshua wants public-ready posture regardless of current visibility. |
| **EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED** (client deliverable, client owns public posture) | **KEEP EXEMPT** — ZestStream voice gate doesn't apply because the surface isn't ZestStream-owned. Client decides their own public posture. Examples: alpsinsurance, mobile-eats client deliverables. |
| **EXEMPT_PUBLIC_FACING** (already public, no scoring needed because already passed gate) | **KEEP** — no-op for already-public surfaces. |

## Precision rule

The directive applies to surfaces where ZestStream brand-voice IS the canonical voice — i.e. flywheel, skillos, zeststream-infra, picoz, polymarket-pico-z, vrtx (Joshua-owned/internal). Client-owned surfaces (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, etc) maintain their EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED status.

## Acceptance gates (UNCHANGED, scope refined)

AG1: Author canonical L-rule PUBLIC-READY-DEFAULT in .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md.
AG2: Update publishability-bar.sh + L89 voice-gate scripts: remove `private_internal` short-circuit, KEEP `EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED` and `EXEMPT_PUBLIC_FACING` short-circuits.
AG3: Re-audit per flywheel-sib04 — only the `private_internal` closes (lzc6, f3s7, u1zd, 1t8t, lzw7), NOT the `EXEMPT_CLIENT_OWNED` closes (wrjv etc).
AG4: Propagate via doctrine-sync 6h cadence — all 57 fleet repos pick up the L-rule + script changes.
AG5: Update L89 trigger language to reflect three-way exemption taxonomy.

## Why this matters

- Optionality: Joshua can flip private→public on Joshua-owned repos without scrambling
- Brand discipline: ZestStream voice + grounding applies to Joshua-owned, not client-owned
- Quality consistency: same bar for same class
- Client respect: client-owned voice posture stays client-owned

description:
## Source

Three consecutive skill-enhance / skillos-new-skill-request beads closed scope-pass with four_lens scores below 7:

- flywheel-spdu (skill-enhance-beads-br) — brand:4,sniff:4,jeff:5,public:3, before:3.54 → after:5.25
- flywheel-2gvl (skillos-new-skill-request mutation-safety-contract) — 4/5/5/4
- flywheel-njzi (skillos-new-skill-request ipc-transport-contract) — 4/5/5/4

ALL THREE workers cited the SAME root cause:
`skill_autoresearch_operational_tooling_python_preference_mismatch`

Diagnostic: skill-autoresearch substrate prefers Python operational tooling but the canonical flywheel surface (canonical-cli-scoping, jsm, beads-br, agent-orchestration) is shell-first. Workers correctly bound scope (no out-of-scope mutations) but the underlying tooling-preference mismatch produces low brand/sniff/public scores because the resulting skill files don't match flywheel surface conventions.

Workers in flywheel-werb (agent-memory) and flywheel-w304 (accretive-cron-orchestration) and flywheel-puwl (agent-orchestration) and flywheel-raq3 (validation-fixture-contract) and flywheel-cmf4 (failure-taxonomy-receipts) all scored 8-9 on the same patterns — so it's not a worker-skill issue, it's a target-skill-class issue.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Audit skill-autoresearch SKILL.md + scripts/ to identify python-vs-shell tooling preference. Document explicitly which substrates skill-autoresearch is suited for vs not.

AG2: Author doctrine page `.flywheel/doctrine/skill-autoresearch-tooling-preference-class.md` capturing: the three failure beads, the root cause, the working pattern (5 beads scored 8-9), the failing pattern (3 beads scored 4-5), and the routing rule (which skill-enhance bead-class needs which substrate).

AG3: Update dispatch packet contract: when bead targets a shell-first skill (canonical-cli-scoping, jsm, beads-br, agent-orchestration), DON'T route through skill-autoresearch. When bead targets a python-friendly skill (skill-builder-managed), skill-autoresearch is fine.

AG4: Re-route the three failed beads (spdu, 2gvl, njzi) — do NOT re-dispatch as-is. Re-author with explicit shell-first tooling guidance OR park as known-pattern-mismatch.

AG5: Propagate doctrine via doctrine-sync 6h cadence to peer orchs (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, skillos) so they don't repeat the same pattern.
title: [doctrine] skill-autoresearch python-vs-shell tooling preference mismatch class

description:
## Joshua observation (2026-05-08T18:55Z)

"When we update our jsm skills - since some of these are jsm skills - they will overwrite the edits we're making here."

## Risk

Workers across waves 13-14 have been directly editing skill files at \`~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md\`. Some of those skills are JSM-managed. The next \`jsm push\` or \`jsm pull --update\` will OVERWRITE the worker's edits with the canonical JSM version.

## What we've seen

Worker discipline IS present in some cases:
- flywheel-irm9 (skill-enhance-cass): worker detected JSM management, produced \`jsm_push_ready_patch=yes\` artifact INSTEAD of direct mutation. CORRECT PATTERN.
- flywheel-jf7c, npo8, 96np, nf7x, ewqr, d7lv: worker reported \`jsm_status=not_installed_or_saved_in_jsm_db\` and edited directly. SAFE for now but unprotected against future JSM enrollment.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: AUDIT — generate report of which \`~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md\` files are JSM-managed vs unmanaged. Use \`jsm list\` and check each skill that received worker edits this session (waves 13-14).

AG2: REVERT-OR-PROMOTE — for each JSM-managed skill that received direct worker edits this session: either revert the live skill (jsm pull) and produce a jsm-push-ready patch instead, OR push the worker's edits via jsm push.

AG3: ENFORCEMENT — extend worker dispatch packet for skill-enhance class:
  - Pre-flight: \`jsm status <skill-name>\` MUST run before any direct skill mutation
  - If JSM-managed: worker MUST produce jsm-push-ready patch artifact (not mutate live)
  - If unmanaged: worker MAY mutate directly, but MUST also write the same patch as a jsm-import-ready artifact in case the skill becomes JSM-managed later

AG4: DOCTRINE — promote this pattern to canonical L-rule (or extend an existing one): skill-enhance work MUST honor JSM management state, with jsm-push-ready-patch as the canonical artifact, not direct mutation.

AG5: REGRESSION — fixture test: skill-enhance dispatch packet to JSM-managed skill MUST refuse direct mutation; dispatch packet to unmanaged skill MUST still produce jsm-push-ready patch as artifact.

## Cross-references

- This session waves 13-14: ewqr, ftbe, irm9, jf7c, npo8, 96np, nf7x, d7lv (all skill-enhance class)
- ~/.claude/skills/jsm/ — JSM skill management substrate
- L62 series (skill management doctrine) — locate canonical L-rule for skill mutation discipline
- flywheel-irm9 callback — shows the correct jsm_push_ready_patch=yes pattern
title: [CRITICAL] JSM-managed skills will overwrite our worker edits — audit + jsm-push-ready-patch discipline needed

description:
## Source

skillos:1 / BrightLake cross-orch coordination 2026-05-08T18:35Z. Severity: high — silent 30+ min freeze observed at skillos:2 today (post-completion, child process hung).

## Two gaps in current detector

**Gap 1: detector doesn't catch background-terminal hangs**
Current `codex-template-stuck-detector.sh` v2 classifies: model_at_capacity_halt, post_completion, input_deaf/buffer_stuck. Missing: pattern `Waiting for background terminal (Xm Ys • esc to interrupt)`. The timer increments so hash-stability check returns alive.

**Gap 2: caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive doesn't exist**
`test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh` exists but detector's `recover()` doesn't invoke `caam activate <next-profile>`.

## Proposed acceptance gates

AG1: Add `background_terminal_stuck` subclass to classify() with `Waiting for background terminal \(([0-9]+)m` pattern + 5min threshold; strip timer text from t0/t1 before sha comparison

AG2: New script `.flywheel/scripts/caam-rotate-and-respawn.sh` orchestrating 6-step rotation:
  1. Read current active CAAM profile (`caam profile current`)
  2. Pick next from rotation roster
  3. `caam activate <next>`
  4. ntm-send Ctrl-C, wait clean prompt
  5. `/flywheel:respawn` on new profile
  6. Re-dispatch last in-flight bead from dispatch-log.jsonl

AG3: Wire `caam-rotate-and-respawn` into detector's `recover()` for both `model_at_capacity_halt` and new `background_terminal_stuck` subclasses

AG4: Doctor extension — add 5 metrics: codex_freeze_recovery_attempted_24h, codex_freeze_recovery_succeeded_24h, codex_freeze_recovery_success_pct_24h, caam_rotation_count_24h, caam_active_profile, caam_profiles_available; WARN if success_pct < 0.5; FAIL if profiles_available < 1

AG5: Tests — fixtures for new subclass + e2e test for rotate-and-respawn

## Effort estimate

- bg-terminal class: ~1 hr
- caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive: ~2-3 hrs
- recover() wire-in: ~30 min
- doctor metrics: ~30 min
- tests: ~1.5 hrs

Total ~5-6 hrs flywheel-side work.

## Real-world impact

skillos:2 freeze observed 2026-05-08T18:30Z: post-completion (PR #69 merged at 0af88c35), spawned bg terminal task, hung 30+ min undetected, Joshua manually respawned. Every active codex worker pane is at risk.

## Cross-references

- `.flywheel/scripts/codex-template-stuck-detector.sh` v2 (extend)
- `.flywheel/tests/test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh` (already exists)
- `.flywheel/scripts/worker-auto-respawn-watchdog.sh` (sibling)
- `~/.claude/skills/caam/SKILL.md`
- `/Users/josh/.local/bin/caam` (rotation CLI)
- AGENTS.md L91, L115 (siblings)
- INCIDENTS fire-and-forget-dispatch 2026-05-08 (same trauma family)
title: [skillos-proposal] codex-stuck-detector bg-terminal class + caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive
